concerns.text: FR-014 (Report associational framing) and FR-017 (Report 'Recall relative to triangulated ground truth') lack explicit implementation tasks. T034 generates the report but does not explicitly mandate the inclusion of the specific 'associational' disclaimer text or the 'LLM-only' recall metric calculation logic defined in the spec.

FR-015 (Define 'CPU-tractable' as ≤7GB memory and ≤5 min latency) is referenced in T019 and T022, but there is no task to implement the actual memory monitoring or latency enforcement logic (e.g., a watchdog or resource limiter) to ensure these hard constraints are met during execution, only to 'load' the model or 'skip' on timeout.

FR-001 requires fetching from '3-5 specified' repositories. T012 mentions fetching from '3-5 target repos' but there is no task to implement the configuration loading/validation of this specific list (e.g., reading from a config file or CLI arg) before execution, risking runtime failure if the list is missing.

Constitution Principle VII (Evaluation Rigor) mandates reporting 'effect sizes' alongside p-values. T032 implements McNemar's and Chi-square tests, but no task explicitly covers the calculation and reporting of effect sizes (e.g., odds ratios, Cramér's V) required by the constitution.

T028 implements 'line-shift tolerance (±5 lines)' which is not defined in FR-012 (which strictly defines Jaccard index on exact line sets). While the Plan mentions this tolerance, the task creates a divergence from the Spec's strict definition without a clear traceability tag to a spec amendment or plan-specific override, risking a 'Single Source of Truth' violation.
